nodes.title: Handle null/undefined as empty text
nodes.why: This keeps rendering stable even when optional children are missing.
nodes.alternative: Throw an error when node is null/undefined.
nodes.tradeoff: More forgiving API, but silently treats empty nodes as blank text.

nodes.title: Render strings/numbers as text nodes
nodes.why: Primitive children should appear as visible text in the DOM tree.
nodes.alternative: Only allow object nodes and ignore primitives.
nodes.tradeoff: Supports real-world mixed children, with one extra type branch.

nodes.title: Create element from node.type
nodes.why: This is the core step that turns the JSON-like node into a real DOM element.
nodes.alternative: Build HTML strings and parse them later.
nodes.tradeoff: DOM API is safer and explicit, but slightly more verbose.

nodes.title: Skip null props and map className to class
nodes.why: Matches common frontend prop shape and avoids writing empty attributes.
nodes.alternative: Apply props directly without key mapping.
nodes.tradeoff: Small mapping logic, better compatibility with UI data.

nodes.title: Render child arrays recursively in order
nodes.why: Depth-first recursion preserves the exact child order from input.
nodes.tradeoff: Recursion is simple and readable, but adds stack depth for deep trees.

nodes.title: Support a single non-array child too
nodes.why: Input can contain one child directly, not always an array.
nodes.alternative: Require every node to use array children.
nodes.tradeoff: More flexible parser with one extra conditional branch.

nodes.title: Return the completed element
nodes.why: Each recursive call returns a node ready to be appended by its parent.
nodes.alternative: Mutate external root state and return nothing.
nodes.tradeoff: Return-based recursion is easy to compose and test.
